title: Our Lady Of The Snows
content:
В В В В A nation spoke to a Nation,
В В В В A Queen sent word to a Throne:
В В В В пїЅDaughter am I in my motherпїЅs house,
В В В В But mistress in my own.
В В В В The gates are mine to open,
В В В В As the gates are mine to close,
В В В В And I set my house in order,пїЅ
В В В В Said our Lady of the Snows.

В В В В пїЅNeither with laughter nor weeping,
В В В В Fear or the childпїЅs amaze,
В В В В Soberly under the White ManпїЅs law
В В В В My white men go their ways.
В В В В Not for the GentilesпїЅ clamour,
В В В В Insult or threat of blows,
В В В В Bow we the knee to Baal,пїЅ
В В В В Said our Lady of the Snows.

В В В В пїЅMy speech is clean and single,
В В В В I talk of common things,
В В В В Words of the wharf and the market-place
В В В В And the ware the merchant brings:
В В В В Favour to those I favour,
В В В В But a stumbling-block to my foes.
В В В В Many there be that hate us,пїЅ
В В В В Said our Lady of the Snows.

В В В В пїЅI called my chiefs to council
В В В В In the din of a troubled year;
В В В В For the sake of a sign ye would not see,
В В В В And a word ye would not hear.
В В В В This is our message and answer;
В В В В This is the path we chose:
В В В В For we be also a people,пїЅ
В В В В Said our Lady of the Snows.

В В В В пїЅCarry the word to my sisters,
В В В В To the Queens of the East and the South.
В В В В I have proven faith in the Heritage
В В В В By more than the word of the mouth.
В В В В They that are wise may follow
В В В В Ere the worldпїЅs war-trumpet blows,,
В В В В But I, I am first in the battle,пїЅ
В В В В Said our Lady of the Snows.

В В В В A Nation spoke to a Nation
В В В В A Throne sent word to a Throne:
В В В В пїЅDaughter am I in my motherпїЅs house
В В В В But mistress in my own.
В В В В The gates are mine to open,
В В В В As the gates are mine to close,
В В В В And I abide by my MotherпїЅs House,пїЅ
В В В В Said our Lady of the Snows.
meaning: About: Joseph Rudyard Kipling (December 30, 1865 пїЅ January 18, 1936) was an English author and poet, born in Bombay, India, and best known for his works The Jungle Book (1894), The Second Jungle Book (1895), Just So Stories (1902), and Puck of Pook's Hill (1906); his novel, Kim (1901); his poems, including Mandalay (1890), Gunga Din (1890), and "IfпїЅ" (1910); and his many short stories, including "The Man Who Would Be King" (1888) and the collections Life's Handicap (1891), The Day's Work (1898), and Plain Tales from the Hills (1888). He is regarded as a major "innovator in the art of the short story"; his children's books are enduring classics of children's literature; and his best work speaks to a versatile and luminous narrative gift.
